content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as hierarchical "curiosity reservoirs", where unused, unlabeled memory slots stored for active query selection act exactly like latent curiosity drive reserves in biologically-inspired agent design. This reframing means that memory scarcity (a core constraint in modern large agent systems) can be repositioned as a feature that amplifies, rather than limits, sustained open-ended exploration.
